name: Spirulina
summary: Spirulina is tracked for Nutrient-dense cyanobacteria with antioxidant and lipid-metabolism mechanisms proposed with conservative evidence framing. Treat dosing and outcomes as review-gated unless PMID-backed human evidence is present.
description: Spirulina is tracked for Nutrient-dense cyanobacteria with antioxidant and lipid-metabolism mechanisms proposed with conservative evidence framing. Treat dosing and outcomes as review-gated unless PMID-backed human evidence is present.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.

claimMap.claim: v9.9: Spirulina evidence exists for lipid/metabolic markers but quality and contamination control are key.
claimMap.notes: v9.9: Spirulina evidence exists for lipid/metabolic markers but quality and contamination control are key.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: 1–8 g/day spirulina used in human studies; dose depends on outcome/product
